_description: Git worktree and lock-safety configuration for Claude Code sessions. This file documents conventions — Claude Code reads this to understand how parallel tasks should be structured.

parallelTaskStrategy.rule: Each parallel code task MUST run in its own git worktree. Never operate multiple tasks simultaneously on the same working directory.
parallelTaskStrategy.how: Claude Code creates worktrees automatically when you use the --worktree flag or when the openclaw harness spawns tasks. Each worktree has an isolated index, so git operations cannot conflict.

rules: NEVER force-remove a lock younger than 5 minutes — another operation may be live
Use scripts/git-safe.sh <git-args> instead of calling git directly in scripts
The pre-commit hook in .git/hooks/pre-commit clears stale locks automatically
If you hit a lock error: check age with `stat .git/index.lock`, then decide

checkBeforeGitOp: If yes: how old is it? (stat -f %m .git/index.lock on macOS)
If older than 5 min: safe to remove
If newer: wait up to 30s, then re-check
